claim: The AI Fluency Framework's four competencies (Delegation, Description, Discernment, Diligence) are applicable across all three interaction modes: automation, augmentation, and agency
actionable: Apply the 4D framework consistently whether using AI for task automation, collaborative augmentation, or independent agent delegation
source_title: Lesson 11: Conclusion | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: Most powerful AI outcomes emerge from identifying opportunities where human expertise and AI capabilities build on each other's strengths through iterative collaboration
actionable: Design AI workflows to leverage complementary human and AI strengths rather than trying to fully automate human judgment or fully manual processes
source_title: Lesson 11: Conclusion | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: Critical evaluation through discernment is a non-negotiable responsibility that protects against AI limitations and enables achievements neither human nor AI could create alone
actionable: Maintain human oversight with structured discernment practices in all AI collaborations to catch limitations and ensure quality
source_title: Lesson 11: Conclusion | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: You remain accountable for what you do with AI and its impact on others; transparency about AI's role in your work builds trust and integrity
actionable: Disclose AI involvement in your work outputs and retain final accountability for quality and appropriateness
source_title: Lesson 11: Conclusion | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course
